entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A period of abnormally dry weather prevalent globally

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: Region where drought is highly prevalent

entities.name: SOUTH-EAST ASIAN COUNTRIES
entities.description: Region where drought is highly prevalent

entities.name: CLIMATIC INSTABILITIES
entities.description: Consistent instabilities in climate that worsen drought situations

entities.name: UNPREDICTABLE RAINFALL PATTERNS
entities.description: Irregular rainfall patterns that worsen drought situations

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A C3 staple cereal and important food crop for the majority of the world's population

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: A major growth retarding threat for rice that affects grain quality and yield

entities.name: GRAIN QUALITY
entities.description: The quality of rice grains, which is reduced by drought stress

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of rice produced, which is reduced by drought stress

entities.name: RICE PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall productivity of rice, which is deteriorated by drought

entities.name: ACCLIMATION RESPONSES
entities.description: Responses induced by drought that aid in stress mitigation

entities.name: STRESS MITIGATION
entities.description: The process of reducing the negative impacts of stress

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of rice plants to withstand and survive drought conditions

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL ADAPTATIONS
entities.description: Biological adaptations used to understand and develop drought tolerance

entities.name: OMICS
entities.description: High-throughput biological analysis used to develop drought-resilient rice

entities.name: TRANSGENIC APPROACHES
entities.description: Genetic modification techniques used to develop drought-tolerant rice

entities.name: MOLECULAR BREEDING
entities.description: Breeding techniques using molecular markers to develop drought-tolerant rice
